L1.area: Distributed inference data transfer and inter-process communication layer

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where a distributed communication component uses unsafe pickle-based serialization to exchange metadata and transfer instructions over network sockets that are bound to all network interfaces. An attacker who can reach these sockets can send crafted serialized payloads to achieve remote code execution.

L3.area: KV cache transfer pipe implementations for distributed inference
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists in a third-party KV cache transfer integration where pickle-based serialization is used to exchange buffer pointers and transfer metadata over ZeroMQ sockets bound to all network interfaces. An attacker who can reach these sockets can send crafted pickle payloads to achieve remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The mooncake integration uses pickle-based serialization (recv_pyobj/send_pyobj) over ZeroMQ sockets bound to all network interfaces, allowing remote code execution via crafted pickle payloads.
